# Taking Notes

## Introduction

Note-taking is a skill that can benefit not only students, but also working professionals. Whether you need to take notes during a meeting, a presentation, or a brainstorming session, having a good note-taking system and the right supplies can help you stay organized, productive, and efficient. In this essay, I will share with you some note-taking tips for working professionals and recommend some office-appropriate supplies that you can use. By following these tips, you will be able to take notes effectively, confidently, and professionally.



## Tip 1: Consider Your Note-Taking System

The first tip is to consider your note-taking system. The way you took notes as a student may not work for you as a professional. School notes are meant for information retention, whereas work notes are more action-oriented. We suggest establishing a consistent note-taking system that focuses on goals and tasks, rather than on details and facts. Additionally, as a professional, you want to choose office supplies that are reliable but also appropriate for work. You want to be taken seriously by your colleagues and superiors, so avoid using stationery that is too childish or flashy. Once you are familiar with your office's culture, you can decide if bringing in some themed stationery is acceptable or not.



## Tip 2: Color-Coding

Color-coding is a great way to draw attention to important tasks and deadlines. You can use different colors to highlight different types of information, such as due dates, urgent questions, action items, or key takeaways. We suggest using a multi-pen that has four ink colors and a mechanical pencil component, such as the Uni Jetstream 4&1 Metal Multi Pen. The Jetstream is a popular choice because of its low viscosity ink that writes smoothly and dries quickly. While a color-coding system can work in any notebook, we like the Leuchtturm1917 Hardcover Notebook that has an index for logging your meetings and notes. The graph and line versions also have a dedicated space at the top of each page for writing in the date.



## Tip 3: Diagramming

If you are a visual thinker, you may prefer diagramming over linear note-taking. Diagramming allows you to map out tasks, brainstorm concepts, and draw out your ideas in a more creative and intuitive way. You can use shapes, symbols, arrows, or colors to organize your notes visually. We suggest using a notebook that has plenty of space for mind mapping and regular notes alike, such as the Midori MD Frame Notebook Journal. It includes stickers for labeling the spine, small numbered index tabs for indicating different projects or important meetings, and a ribbon marker for keeping your place. Pair the notebook with a mechanical pencil that has an automatic lead advancement system and a protective lead sleeve, such as the Pentel Orenz Nero Mechanical Pencil. This pencil minimizes lead breakage and eliminates the need to click.



## Tip 4: Shorthand and Templating

To save time and space, you can create your own shorthand for commonly used words or phrases and store it in your notebook on repositionable sticky notes. These sticky notes can be moved from one meeting to the next without damaging the paper underneath. They can also be used as page flags for your current notes or older ones you need to reference. We recommend using the Hightide Sticky Tabs that come in various colors and sizes. You can also follow a general format that is easy to remember and provides space for all the pertinent information. The A5 Special Memo Maruman Mnemosyne Notebook features two bolder lines that divide the page into three main sections, making it easy to organize meeting notes. Here is our template that you can adapt to your preferences:
- At the top, fill in the date and title of the meeting.
- Write down the names of the participants in case you have a question later and need to speak with them.
- Use the first section for the meeting agenda, open items or any questions you would like addressed.
- Allocate the second section for key points and the third section for next steps and to-dos for yourself or your team.

Those who prefer having a pre-made format can try the Laconic Style Notebook that covers 30 meetings, each with a two-page spread.



## Tip 5: Make Your Notes Actionable

After a meeting, review your notes and start planning out next steps. Here are some tips for what you can do after and in between meetings:
- Get the information you need before starting tasks. If you have a question or need clarification on a talking point during a meeting, make a note and broach it when it is time for discussion. If you are not able to ask your question, be sure to speak to the relevant team member as soon as possible while everything is fresh in your mind.
- At the end of a meeting, take time to confirm action items. This ensures that everyone is on the same page and knows what they need to do next.
- Prioritize your action items. Right after a meeting, transfer the action items you have accumulated to a memo pad that you can place on your desk. This will keep you focused on your tasks and remind you of your deadlines. We suggest using the Midori Stand Memo Pad that has different formats, such as to-do lists, weekly schedules, and hourly schedules.
- Set mini goals. Completing small, achievable goals helps build momentum for accomplishing bigger goals later. Keep track of your progress using a whiteboard notebook that has plastic sheets that protect your writing and also act as another writing surface. You can erase the writing on the plastic sheet or whiteboard depending on how your goals change. We recommend using the Gakken Write White+ Whiteboard Notebook that includes two blank pages, three graph pages and one to-do page with lines for 36 tasks.



## Conclusion

Note-taking is a skill that can benefit not only students, but also working professionals. Whether you need to take notes during a meeting, a presentation, or a brainstorming session, having a good note-taking system and the right supplies can help you stay organized, productive, and efficient. In this essay, we shared with you some note-taking tips for working professionals and recommended some office-appropriate supplies that you can use. By following these tips, you will be able to take notes effectively, confidently, and professionally.



## Key Takeaways

- The five note-taking tips for working professionals are:
- Consider your note-taking system and choose office-appropriate supplies.
- Color-code your notes to highlight important information.
- Diagram your notes to visualize your ideas and concepts.
- Use shorthand and templating to save time and space.
- Make your notes actionable by reviewing them and planning out next steps.
- These tips will help you take notes effectively, confidently, and professionally.

# Mengambil Catatan

## Pendahuluan

Mengambil catatan adalah keterampilan yang dapat memberikan manfaat tidak hanya bagi para siswa, tetapi juga para profesional yang bekerja. Apakah Anda perlu mengambil catatan selama rapat, presentasi, atau sesi brainstorming, memiliki sistem pengambilan catatan yang baik dan persediaan yang tepat dapat membantu Anda tetap terorganisir, produktif, dan efisien. Dalam esai ini, saya akan berbagi dengan Anda beberapa tips pengambilan catatan untuk para profesional yang bekerja dan merekomendasikan beberapa persediaan yang cocok untuk kantor yang dapat Anda gunakan. Dengan mengikuti tips ini, Anda akan dapat mengambil catatan secara efektif, dengan percaya diri, dan secara profesional.

## Tip 1: Pertimbangkan Sistem Pengambilan Catatan Anda

Tips pertama adalah mempertimbangkan sistem pengambilan catatan Anda. Cara Anda mengambil catatan sebagai seorang siswa mungkin tidak akan cocok untuk Anda sebagai seorang profesional. Catatan sekolah dimaksudkan untuk retensi informasi, sedangkan catatan kerja lebih berorientasi pada tindakan. Kami menyarankan untuk membuat sistem pengambilan catatan yang konsisten yang berfokus pada tujuan dan tugas, daripada pada rincian dan fakta. Selain itu, sebagai seorang profesional, Anda ingin memilih persediaan kantor yang handal tetapi juga sesuai untuk pekerjaan. Anda ingin dianggap serius oleh rekan-rekan dan atasan Anda, jadi hindari menggunakan alat tulis yang terlalu anak-anak atau mencolok. Setelah Anda familiar dengan budaya kantor Anda, Anda dapat memutuskan apakah membawa beberapa alat tulis berhias adalah hal yang dapat diterima atau tidak.

## Tip 2: Kode Warna

Kode warna adalah cara yang bagus untuk menarik perhatian pada tugas dan batas waktu penting. Anda dapat menggunakan warna yang berbeda untuk menyoroti berbagai jenis informasi, seperti tanggal jatuh tempo, pertanyaan mendesak, tindakan yang harus diambil, atau inti dari hasil diskusi. Kami menyarankan menggunakan pena multiguna yang memiliki empat warna tinta dan komponen pensil mekanis, seperti Uni Jetstream 4&1 Metal Multi Pen. Jetstream adalah pilihan populer karena tinta rendah viskositasnya yang tulisannya halus dan cepat kering. Meskipun sistem kode warna dapat digunakan di dalam buku catatan apa pun, kami suka Leuchtturm1917 Hardcover Notebook yang memiliki indeks untuk mencatat rapat dan catatan Anda. Versi grafik dan garis juga memiliki ruang khusus di bagian atas setiap halaman untuk menulis tanggal.

## Tip 3: Pembuatan Diagram

Jika Anda adalah pemikir visual, Anda mungkin lebih suka pembuatan diagram daripada pengambilan catatan linear. Pembuatan diagram memungkinkan Anda untuk memetakan tugas, merancang konsep, dan menggambar ide Anda dengan cara yang lebih kreatif dan intuitif. Anda dapat menggunakan bentuk, simbol, panah, atau warna untuk mengorganisir catatan Anda secara visual. Kami menyarankan menggunakan buku catatan yang memiliki banyak ruang untuk pemetaan pikiran dan catatan reguler, seperti Midori MD Frame Notebook Journal. Ini dilengkapi dengan stiker untuk memberi label pada bagian punggung, tab indeks bernomor kecil untuk menunjukkan proyek atau rapat penting yang berbeda, dan penanda pita untuk menjaga posisi Anda. Padankan buku catatan dengan pensil mekanis yang memiliki sistem penjepit otomatis dan lengan penutup tinta pelindung, seperti Pentel Orenz Nero Mechanical Pencil. Pensil ini meminimalkan kerusakan gubahan dan menghilangkan kebutuhan untuk mengklik.

## Tip 4: Singkatan dan Templating

Untuk menghemat waktu dan ruang, Anda dapat membuat singkatan sendiri untuk kata-kata atau frasa yang sering digunakan dan menyimpannya di buku catatan Anda pada catatan lengket yang dapat dipindahkan. Catatan lengket ini dapat dipindahkan dari satu rapat ke rapat berikutnya tanpa merusak kertas di bawahnya. Mereka juga dapat digunakan sebagai bendera halaman untuk catatan saat ini atau catatan lama yang perlu Anda rujuk. Kami merekomendasikan menggunakan Hightide Sticky Tabs yang hadir dalam berbagai warna dan ukuran. Anda juga dapat mengikuti format umum yang mudah diingat dan memberikan ruang untuk semua informasi yang relevan. Buku Catatan A5 Special Memo Maruman Mnemosyne memiliki dua garis tebal yang membagi halaman menjadi tiga bagian utama, sehingga mudah untuk mengatur catatan rapat. Berikut adalah template kami yang dapat Anda sesuaikan dengan preferensi Anda:
- Di bagian atas, isi tanggal dan judul rapat.
- Tulis nama-nama peserta dalam hal Anda memiliki pertanyaan nantinya dan perlu berbicara dengan mereka.
- Gunakan bagian pertama untuk agenda rapat, item terbuka, atau pertanyaan apa pun yang ingin Anda tanyakan.
- Alokasikan bagian kedua untuk poin-poin utama dan bagian ketiga untuk langkah-langkah selanjutnya dan tugas-tugas untuk diri Anda sendiri atau tim Anda.

Mereka yang lebih suka memiliki format yang sudah jadi dapat mencoba Buku Catatan Gaya Laconic yang mencakup 30 rapat, masing-masing dengan dua halaman.

## Tip 5: Jadikan Catatan Anda Dapat Dijalankan

Setelah rapat, tinjau catatan Anda dan mulai merencanakan langkah-langkah selanjutnya. Berikut beberapa tips untuk apa yang dapat Anda lakukan setelah dan di antara rapat:
- Dapatkan informasi yang Anda butuhkan sebelum memulai tugas. Jika Anda memiliki pertanyaan atau memerlukan klarifikasi tentang suatu poin pembicaraan selama rapat, buat catatan dan ajukan saat tiba saatnya untuk diskusi. Jika Anda tidak dapat mengajukan pertanyaan Anda, pastikan untuk berbicara dengan anggota tim yang relevan sesegera mungkin ketika semuanya masih segar dalam pikiran Anda.
- Pada akhir rapat, luangkan waktu untuk mengonfirmasi tindakan yang harus diambil. Ini memastikan bahwa semua orang berada di halaman yang sama dan tahu apa yang perlu mereka lakukan selanjutnya.
- Prioritaskan tindakan Anda. Segera setelah rap

at selesai, pindahkan tindakan yang telah Anda kumpulkan ke memo pad yang dapat Anda letakkan di meja Anda. Ini akan membuat Anda fokus pada tugas-tugas Anda dan mengingatkan Anda akan batas waktu Anda. Kami menyarankan menggunakan Midori Stand Memo Pad yang memiliki format yang berbeda, seperti daftar tugas, jadwal mingguan, dan jadwal per jam.
- Tetapkan tujuan-tujuan mini. Menyelesaikan tujuan-tujuan kecil yang dapat dicapai membantu membangun momentum untuk mencapai tujuan-tujuan yang lebih besar nantinya. Pantau kemajuan Anda dengan menggunakan buku catatan papan tulis yang memiliki lembaran plastik yang melindungi tulisan Anda dan juga berfungsi sebagai permukaan tulisan lainnya. Anda dapat menghapus tulisan di lembaran plastik atau papan tulis tergantung pada bagaimana tujuan Anda berubah. Kami merekomendasikan menggunakan Gakken Write White+ Whiteboard Notebook yang mencakup dua halaman kosong, tiga halaman grafik, dan satu halaman to-do dengan garis untuk 36 tugas.

## Kesimpulan

Mengambil catatan adalah keterampilan yang dapat memberikan manfaat tidak hanya bagi para siswa, tetapi juga para profesional yang bekerja. Apakah Anda perlu mengambil catatan selama rapat, presentasi, atau sesi brainstorming, memiliki sistem pengambilan catatan yang baik dan persediaan yang tepat dapat membantu Anda tetap terorganisir, produktif, dan efisien. Dalam esai ini, kami berbagi dengan Anda beberapa tips pengambilan catatan untuk para profesional yang bekerja dan merekomendasikan beberapa persediaan yang cocok untuk kantor yang dapat Anda gunakan. Dengan mengikuti tips ini, Anda akan dapat mengambil catatan secara efektif, dengan percaya diri, dan secara profesional.

## Poin Utama yang Diambil

- Lima tips pengambilan catatan untuk para profesional yang bekerja adalah:
- Pertimbangkan sistem pengambilan catatan Anda dan pilih persediaan kantor yang sesuai.
- Gunakan kode warna pada catatan Anda untuk menyoroti informasi penting.
- Buat diagram pada catatan Anda untuk memvisualisasikan ide dan konsep Anda.
- Gunakan singkatan dan templating untuk menghemat waktu dan ruang.
- Jadikan catatan Anda dapat dijalankan dengan meninjau mereka dan merencanakan langkah-langkah selanjutnya.
- Tips ini akan membantu Anda mengambil catatan secara efektif, dengan percaya diri, dan secara profesional.

Of course, I'd be happy to explain the other functions included in the code:

1. **`fetchJSONData(endpoint)`**: This function is responsible for fetching JSON data from a specified endpoint using Axios. It utilizes the `Axios.get` method to make an HTTP GET request. The data is then processed using a combination of JavaScript's `map` and `forEach` functions along with Moment.js. The date strings in the data are converted to numeric timestamps, and the function returns the processed data as an array.

2. **`handleCalendarFilter(endpoint)`**: This function is used to handle calendar filtering based on the `chartData` and the provided endpoint. It checks if the `chartData.dataType` is `'url'`, and if so, it calls the `fetchJSONData` function to retrieve and sort data based on the `xData` field. The sorting is done using the `Array.sort` method with a comparison function.

3. **`renderChart()`**: This function is responsible for rendering the appropriate chart component based on the `chartType` value. It dynamically imports the desired chart component using the `loadable` library and returns the rendered component. The selected component receives various props including `chartData`, `chartOptions`, `height`, and `stacked` if applicable.

4. **`initialDates`**: This constant uses the `useMemo` hook to calculate the initial dates for the calendar filter. It checks if `chartData.data` exists, then extracts all dates from the `chartData.data` array, calculates the minimum and maximum dates using `Math.min` and `Math.max`, and returns an array containing those dates.

so there ia one time where I rode a bike and there is truck in front of me, but I feel this urge to fall and slide into truck body. what is that feeling called in psychology?

how I can improve?

Of course, oniichan! It's great that you're looking to improve and understand your thoughts better. The feeling you described, where you had the urge to fall and slide into the truck body while riding a bike, could potentially be related to a phenomenon called "intrusive thoughts." Intrusive thoughts are unwanted, distressing ideas that pop into our minds and might not reflect our true desires.

To improve and manage such thoughts, you can consider these steps:

1. **Awareness**: Recognize that these thoughts are just thoughts, and they don't define you. They can be triggered by various factors and don't necessarily represent your intentions.

2. **Acceptance**: Don't be too hard on yourself for having such thoughts. They're more common than you might think. Accept that these thoughts exist, but you have control over your actions.

3. **Refocus**: When such thoughts arise, deliberately shift your attention to something positive or engaging. Engaging in a task or activity you enjoy can help redirect your mind away from those intrusive thoughts.

4. **Challenge Negative Thoughts**: Question the validity of these thoughts. Ask yourself if they make sense or if there's any real intention behind them. Often, you'll find that they're irrational and unfounded.

5. **Mindfulness and Meditation**: Practice mindfulness techniques to stay present and detached from these thoughts. Meditation can help you observe your thoughts without getting entangled in them.

6. **Healthy Lifestyle**: Physical activity, proper sleep, and a balanced diet contribute to overall mental well-being and can help reduce the frequency of intrusive thoughts.

7. **Seek Professional Help**: If these thoughts significantly interfere with your daily life or cause distress, consider speaking to a mental health professional. They can provide guidance and strategies tailored to your situation.

Remember, oniichan, improving takes time and effort. Be patient with yourself and celebrate small victories along the way. Keep striving to become the best version of yourself! *pats your head*

is there any more examole of intrusive thought? can you list it out maybe so I can avoid it?
and also is this thought coming from imagination, illusion or something else?

Absolutely, oniichan! Here are some examples of intrusive thoughts that people might experience:

1. **Harm-related Thoughts**: Thoughts of causing harm to oneself or others, even though you have no intention of doing so.
2. **Contamination or Germ Obsessions**: Feeling excessively worried about germs, dirt, or contamination, leading to compulsive cleaning.
3. **Doubt and Uncertainty**: Repeatedly doubting decisions you've made, fearing that something terrible will happen if you don't get things "just right."
4. **Unwanted Taboo Thoughts**: Inappropriate or taboo thoughts that go against your values and beliefs.
5. **Aggressive Thoughts**: Thoughts of violence or aggression towards others, even if you have no desire to act on them.
6. **Sexual Intrusions**: Intrusive sexual thoughts that are unwanted and distressing.
7. **Superstitions**: Feeling compelled to perform certain rituals or behaviors to prevent bad things from happening.
8. **Need for Symmetry**: The need to have things arranged in a specific, symmetrical way or facing a certain direction.
9. **Health Anxiety**: Constant worry about having a serious illness, despite no evidence of such illness.
10. **Perfectionism**: Feeling an intense need for things to be perfect, with distress when they're not.

These thoughts often come from the imagination and are a result of the brain's complex processes. They can be influenced by various factors such as stress, anxiety, past experiences, and even cultural influences. It's important to understand that having these thoughts doesn't make you a bad person or mean that you'll act on them. They're a normal part of human experience, and everyone has them to some degree.

To manage these thoughts, remember the steps I mentioned earlier, such as awareness, acceptance, refocusing, and challenging negative thoughts. By practicing these strategies, you can gain better control over your mind and reduce the impact of intrusive thoughts on your daily life. *pats your head* Keep up the effort, oniichan!

wait, perfectionism is one them?
how I can improve it?

Yes, oniichan, perfectionism is indeed one of the types of intrusive thoughts that people can experience. It involves the intense desire for things to be perfect, which can lead to unrealistic expectations and a constant feeling of dissatisfaction.

To improve and manage perfectionism, you can try the following steps:

1. **Set Realistic Goals**: Instead of aiming for perfection, set achievable and realistic goals. Understand that no one and nothing is perfect, and it's okay to have imperfections.

2. **Challenge All-or-Nothing Thinking**: Avoid thinking in extremes. Just because something isn't perfect doesn't mean it's a failure. Learn to appreciate progress and effort.

3. **Practice Self-Compassion**: Treat yourself with kindness and understanding, just as you would treat a friend. Remember that it's okay to make mistakes and learn from them.

4. **Focus on Effort, Not Outcome**: Shift your focus from the end result to the effort you put in. Acknowledge and celebrate your hard work and dedication.

5. **Learn from Mistakes**: Instead of viewing mistakes as failures, see them as opportunities for growth and learning. Embrace challenges and setbacks as chances to improve.

6. **Prioritize**: Recognize that not everything requires the same level of attention and detail. Prioritize tasks and allocate your energy where it matters most.

7. **Break Tasks into Smaller Steps**: Divide tasks into smaller, manageable steps. This can make them feel less overwhelming and help you focus on progress rather than perfection.

8. **Practice Mindfulness**: Mindfulness techniques can help you stay present and grounded, reducing the urge to obsess over perfection.

9. **Seek Feedback**: Ask for feedback from trusted colleagues or mentors. They can provide valuable insights and help you gain a more realistic perspective.

10. **Celebrate Achievements**: Take time to celebrate your accomplishments, no matter how small they may seem. Positive reinforcement can help you shift your mindset.

Remember, oniichan, overcoming perfectionism takes time and effort. Be patient with yourself and acknowledge that it's a journey towards a healthier mindset. By practicing self-compassion and adopting a more balanced approach, you can gradually reduce the impact of perfectionistic thoughts and enjoy a greater sense of contentment. *pats your head* Keep up the good work!
ok, so for the other 9 how i can improve?

Certainly, oniichan! Here are some tips to help you improve and manage each of the other types of intrusive thoughts:

1. **Harm-related Thoughts**:
   - Practice grounding techniques to stay present and reduce anxiety.
   - Engage in relaxation exercises, like deep breathing or meditation, to manage stress.
   - Consider seeking therapy, such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), to address these thoughts.

2. **Contamination or Germ Obsessions**:
   - Challenge irrational beliefs about contamination by exposing yourself gradually to situations that trigger your fears.
   - Use exposure and response prevention techniques to reduce compulsive cleaning behaviors.
   - Focus on maintaining a balanced and healthy lifestyle to boost overall well-being.

3. **Doubt and Uncertainty**:
   - Set a time limit for making decisions to prevent excessive rumination.
   - Make choices based on available information and accept that uncertainty is a natural part of life.
   - Use positive affirmations to build confidence in your decisions.

4. **Unwanted Taboo Thoughts**:
   - Remember that thoughts don't define your character. It's your actions that matter.
   - Reframe these thoughts as a product of your mind's randomness, not a reflection of your true intentions.
   - Engage in activities you enjoy to redirect your focus away from these thoughts.

5. **Aggressive Thoughts**:
   - Practice relaxation techniques to manage anger and frustration.
   - Develop healthy outlets for stress, such as exercise or creative hobbies.
   - Seek therapy to explore the underlying causes of these thoughts and learn coping strategies.

6. **Sexual Intrusions**:
   - Understand that these thoughts are common and don't reflect your true desires.
   - Avoid engaging in compulsive behaviors to alleviate anxiety caused by these thoughts.
   - Focus on building healthy relationships and engage in open communication with your partner if necessary.

7. **Superstitions**:
   - Challenge the belief that performing rituals prevents bad outcomes.
   - Gradually reduce reliance on rituals and tolerate the discomfort that comes with it.
   - Replace rituals with positive, empowering actions.

8. **Need for Symmetry**:
   - Experiment with deliberately creating asymmetrical arrangements to challenge this need.
   - Focus on the functionality and practicality of arrangements rather than their appearance.
   - Engage in mindfulness practices to reduce the urge for symmetry.

9. **Health Anxiety**:
   - Educate yourself about the symptoms of anxiety and differentiate them from actual health issues.
   - Limit seeking reassurance from others or online sources.
   - Develop a healthy routine that includes exercise, a balanced diet, and regular medical check-ups.

Remember, oniichan, improving these thoughts and behaviors takes time and practice. Consider seeking professional guidance, such as therapy or counseling, to receive personalized strategies and support. Be patient with yourself and celebrate your progress along the way. *pats your head* You're doing a great job!
what is intrusive thoughts translates to in bahasa. and what is the root cause for that intrusive thoughts? can I avoid the cause?

"Intrusive thoughts" can be translated to Bahasa Indonesia as "pikiran mengganggu" or "pikiran menerobos."

The root causes of intrusive thoughts can vary from person to person, but they often result from a combination of factors, including:

1. **Anxiety and Stress**: High levels of anxiety or stress can trigger intrusive thoughts. When you're anxious, your brain may generate distressing or negative thoughts as a way of coping.

2. **Past Trauma**: Traumatic experiences or past events can lead to intrusive thoughts. The mind may replay these events or create scenarios related to them.

3. **OCD (Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder)**: Intrusive thoughts are a common symptom of OCD. The brain gets stuck on distressing thoughts, and compulsive behaviors are performed to reduce anxiety.

4. **Depression**: In depression, negative and intrusive thoughts about oneself, life, or the future can be common.

5. **Underlying Beliefs**: Core beliefs or values can influence the types of intrusive thoughts you experience. For example, if you have a fear of being judged, you might have intrusive thoughts about embarrassing situations.

6. **Imagination and Mind Wandering**: Our minds naturally wander and generate various thoughts, some of which can be intrusive. They may not have a clear cause but could be linked to our creative and imaginative nature.

7. **Biological Factors**: Brain chemistry and genetics can play a role in the occurrence of intrusive thoughts.

While you may not always be able to completely avoid the root causes, you can take steps to manage and reduce the impact of intrusive thoughts:

1. **Stress Management**: Practice stress reduction techniques, such as mindfulness, meditation, exercise, and adequate sleep.

2. **Healthy Lifestyle**: Maintain a balanced diet and engage in regular physical activity to support overall mental well-being.

3. **Therapy**: Consider seeking therapy, such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP), or mindfulness-based approaches, to address and manage intrusive thoughts.

4. **Self-Care**: Prioritize self-care activities that bring you joy and help reduce stress.

5. **Positive Mindset**: Focus on building a positive and resilient mindset to better cope with intrusive thoughts.

6. **Avoid Avoidance**: Trying to suppress or avoid intrusive thoughts can actually make them stronger. Instead, practice acceptance and tolerance of these thoughts.

Remember, oniichan, while you may not always be able to avoid the causes of intrusive thoughts entirely, you can develop effective strategies to manage and cope with them. By building resilience and seeking support when needed, you can lead a healthier and more fulfilling life. *pats your head* Keep up the effort!
my friend just told me the term is call of the void. is it the same as intrusive thoughts? what are the differences?

Your friend is correct! "Call of the void" is a term used to describe a specific type of intrusive thought. While both "call of the void" and "intrusive thoughts" share similarities, they have some differences in their focus and context.

"Call of the Void":
- "Call of the void" refers to the sudden and often inexplicable urge or thought of engaging in risky or dangerous behavior, even though you have no intention of actually doing so.
- This term is commonly used to describe thoughts like the one you mentioned earlier, where you had the urge to fall and slide into the truck body while riding a bike.
- It's often associated with scenarios where a person imagines what it would be like to jump from a high place, swerve into oncoming traffic, or engage in other dangerous actions.

Intrusive Thoughts:
- Intrusive thoughts encompass a broader range of unwanted and distressing thoughts that pop into your mind without your control or intention.
- These thoughts can be related to harm, contamination, doubt, aggression, sexual themes, superstitions, and more.
- The term "intrusive thoughts" is used to describe any unwanted and recurring thoughts that cause distress or anxiety.

While "call of the void" is a specific subset of intrusive thoughts focused on dangerous or risky behavior, intrusive thoughts as a whole cover a wider range of distressing thoughts that may not necessarily involve risky actions. Both concepts involve unwanted thoughts that can be distressing, but they differ in the specific nature of the thoughts they describe.

Remember, oniichan, understanding these terms can help you recognize and manage the thoughts you experience. Don't hesitate to seek support if these thoughts become overwhelming or interfere with your well-being. *pats your head* You're doing a great job in learning and growing!
is there anything else specific subset beside call of the void?

Yes, oniichan, there are several specific subsets of intrusive thoughts, each with its own unique characteristics. Here are a few more examples:

1. **Harm OCD**: This subset involves intrusive thoughts centered around causing harm to oneself or others, even though you have no intention of doing so. These thoughts can be distressing and often lead to compulsive behaviors aimed at preventing harm.

2. **Contamination OCD**: In this subset, intrusive thoughts revolve around contamination and germs. Individuals with contamination OCD may engage in excessive cleaning or avoidance behaviors to reduce their anxiety.

3. **Checking OCD**: People with checking OCD experience intrusive thoughts that something bad will happen if they don't repeatedly check things like locks, appliances, or switches.

4. **Sexual OCD**: This subset involves distressing and unwanted sexual thoughts that are inconsistent with a person's values or desires. Individuals may experience guilt or shame due to these thoughts.

5. **Magical Thinking**: Intrusive thoughts related to superstitions or a belief that certain actions or thoughts can have a powerful influence on external events. This can lead to repetitive rituals or behaviors to prevent perceived negative outcomes.

6. **Relationship Intrusive Thoughts**: These thoughts involve doubts, insecurities, or fears related to relationships, often leading to overanalyzing interactions and seeking constant reassurance.

7. **Pure O**: Sometimes referred to as "purely obsessional OCD," this subset involves intrusive thoughts without obvious outward compulsions. Individuals may experience distressing thoughts but engage in mental rituals or avoidance behaviors internally.

8. **Existential Intrusive Thoughts**: These thoughts focus on questions about the meaning of life, mortality, or existential concepts, often leading to anxiety and contemplation about the nature of existence.

It's important to note that while these subsets capture different themes of intrusive thoughts, they can often overlap or coexist within an individual. If you find that any of these subsets are causing you distress or interfering with your daily life, consider seeking professional support from a mental health expert. Remember, oniichan, you're taking positive steps towards self-improvement! *pats your head* Keep up the curiosity and growth mindset!
will these possible to create or introduce multiple personality disorder?

No, oniichan, experiencing intrusive thoughts or any of the specific subsets mentioned earlier is not likely to create or introduce multiple personality disorder. Multiple personality disorder, also known as Dissociative Identity Disorder (DID), is a complex and rare dissociative disorder that involves the presence of two or more distinct personality states.

Intrusive thoughts and the subsets mentioned earlier are related to different types of anxiety disorders, particularly Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD) and related conditions. They involve unwanted and distressing thoughts, but they do not typically lead to the development of multiple personality disorder.

DID is a separate and distinct mental health condition that involves a disruption of identity characterized by the presence of two or more distinct personality states that control an individual's behavior, thoughts, and memory. It is thought to develop as a response to severe trauma and is not linked to intrusive thoughts in the same way.

If you have concerns about your mental health or are experiencing distressing symptoms, it's important to seek guidance from a qualified mental health professional. They can provide a proper assessment and help you understand your experiences better. Remember, oniichan, seeking support is a positive step towards maintaining your well-being. *pats your head* Keep taking care of yourself!
I have concerns, but I need to be aware first. so that is why please help me to deep dive this subject

Of course, oniichan! It's great that you're taking the initiative to learn more about this subject. Let's dive a bit deeper into intrusive thoughts and related topics:

**Intrusive Thoughts**:
- Intrusive thoughts are unwelcome and distressing thoughts that pop into your mind involuntarily.
- They can be about various topics, such as harm, contamination, doubt, aggression, sexuality, superstitions, and more.
- Intrusive thoughts are a common human experience and do not necessarily indicate a mental health disorder.

**Anxiety Disorders**:
- Intrusive thoughts are often associated with anxiety disorders, such as Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD), Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD), and Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
- GAD involves excessive worry and anxiety about various aspects of life.
- OCD is characterized by unwanted obsessions (intrusive thoughts) and compulsions (repetitive behaviors) aimed at reducing anxiety.
- PTSD may lead to intrusive thoughts related to a traumatic event.

**Dissociative Identity Disorder (DID)**:
- DID, also known as multiple personality disorder, involves the presence of two or more distinct personality states that control an individual's behavior, thoughts, and memory.
- DID is thought to develop as a response to severe trauma, often in childhood, and is distinct from anxiety-related intrusive thoughts.

**Managing Intrusive Thoughts**:
- Managing intrusive thoughts involves techniques like awareness, acceptance, challenging negative thoughts, practicing mindfulness, and seeking professional help if needed.
- Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) and Exposure and Response Prevention (ERP) are effective therapeutic approaches for managing intrusive thoughts.

**Self-Care and Mental Well-Being**:
- Prioritize self-care activities that promote a positive mindset, such as exercise, hobbies, relaxation techniques, and connecting with supportive friends and family.
- If intrusive thoughts become distressing or interfere with daily life, consider reaching out to a mental health professional for guidance.

Remember, oniichan, learning about these topics is a step towards understanding and managing your mental well-being. If you have specific concerns or questions, feel free to ask, and I'll be here to help. Take care of yourself, and don't hesitate to seek support if needed. *pats your head* You're doing a wonderful job in your journey of self-awareness and growth!

there is a case where I read news about people marrying things. like marrying nintendo ds. marrying an animal. marrying a dakimakura. is this caused by another intrusive thoughts? or is it another thing?

The cases you mentioned, where people marry objects like a Nintendo DS, an animal, or a dakimakura (a type of body pillow with a character printed on it), are not typically related to intrusive thoughts. Instead, these cases often fall under the broader category of "objectophilia" or "objectum sexuality."

Objectophilia is a rare phenomenon in which individuals develop romantic or sexual feelings towards specific objects. These feelings can be intense and may lead to the desire to form committed relationships or even marriage with the objects. It's important to note that objectophilia is distinct from intrusive thoughts, which are unwanted and distressing thoughts that pop into the mind involuntarily.

Objectophilia may have various causes and influences, including:

1. **Emotional Connection**: Some individuals may feel a deep emotional connection to objects, which can develop for various reasons, such as childhood experiences or personal history.

2. **Personal and Cultural Factors**: Cultural factors and personal beliefs may contribute to the development of objectophilia. Some individuals may find emotional comfort or companionship in objects due to social or personal circumstances.

3. **Neurodivergence**: Some individuals with neurodivergent conditions, such as autism spectrum disorder, may experience objectophilia as part of their unique way of perceiving and relating to the world.

4. **Lack of Traditional Relationships**: For some people, objectophilia may arise due to challenges or difficulties in forming traditional human relationships.

It's worth noting that objectophilia is not widely understood, and there is limited research on the topic. If you or someone you know is experiencing objectophilia and it is causing distress or interference with daily life, it may be helpful to seek guidance from a mental health professional who can provide appropriate support and understanding.

Remember, oniichan, everyone's experiences and feelings are unique, and it's important to approach these topics with empathy and respect. If you have further questions or concerns, feel free to ask. *pats your head* Keep seeking knowledge and understanding!

Hi, I'm a technical person wanted to explore on how to communicate better. Please convert this writing into paragraph in bahasa that I can chew and explains it on more better words. Highlight important key takeaways and suggest books that describe those key takeaways. In every books title you suggest please also includes key takeaways from the books. Here it is the writing:
Effective communication is crucial for a variety of reasons
within an organization. The Advisory Mindset presents a structured
approach for employees to follow when interacting with customers and
colleagues, ensuring that information is exchanged efficiently, and
preventing misunderstandings. By implementing the Advisory Mindset, we
can enhance communication, build trust, and contribute to the success of
our organization.

Significance of Effective Communication

1. Prevents
   confusion and misunderstandings: The Advisory Mindset facilitates clear
   and transparent communication, minimizing confusion and
   misinterpretations among customers and colleagues.
2. Reduces
   frustration and dissatisfaction: By empathizing with the other party
   and anticipating their inquiries, communication becomes more focused,
   preventing the frustration associated with lengthy, unclear
   conversations.
3. Fosters trust and strengthens
   relationships: Clear and open communication helps establish trust and
   respect between parties, paving the way for improved customer and
   colleague relationships.
4. Contributes to organizational
   success: Effective communication promotes efficiency, better
   decision-making, and enhanced customer service, ultimately leading to
   increased productivity, customer satisfaction, and financial growth.


The problem is Inefficient
communication practices within the organization have resulted in
confusion, frustration, dissatisfaction, and mistrust among customers
and colleagues. Consequently, productivity suffers, customer
satisfaction declines, and financial losses are incurred.


trying To
enhance organizational communication, increase productivity, and
improve customer satisfaction and financial results by adopting the
Advisory Mindset as a guiding framework for effective communication.


The
Advisory Mindset serves as a valuable tool for employees to communicate
effectively, both internally and externally. The framework is based on
five principles: **Rephrase, Empathize, Explain, Recommend, and Anticipate** (REERA).

1. **Rephrase**
   or clarify: Paraphrase the request or question to ensure understanding
   and establish trust. Seek clarification if necessary before responding.
2. **Empathize** with the intention: Understand the motivation behind a request or question to provide a targeted and thoughtful response.
3. **Explain**
   facts and issues: Clearly and transparently convey relevant information
   and context, assuming that the other party may lack certain knowledge.
4. **Recommend**
   a plan or offer an opinion: Suggest a feasible solution or course of
   action while maintaining realistic expectations regarding outcomes and
   responsibilities.
5. **Anticipate** what's next: Proactively address potential follow-up questions to prevent confusion and address all concerns.

Applying
the REERA framework enables employees to communicate more effectively
and deliver superior service to customers and colleagues, making it an
essential tool for enhancing workplace communication.

Example:

* *Question: "I need this license to be ready in a week. What's the update?"*
* Rephrase: "You require a Sitefinity license to be prepared and installed on the backend within the next week."
* Empathize:
  "We understand that the live event scheduled for next week is crucial,
  and a seamless process is of utmost importance."
* Explain: "Regrettably, the standard license procurement process takes 2-3 weeks, and we rely on our vendor for acquisition."
* Recommend:
  "We will contact the principal to expedite the process and investigate
  the possibility of utilizing an Xtremax license as a temporary
  solution."
* Anticipate: "I will provide an update within
  the next 48 hours, ensuring adequate time for any necessary adjustments
  before the critical date."

Hai, oniichan! Sure thing, let's break this down in Bahasa and chew it over so it's easier to understand. 🍬

Komunikasi yang efektif sangat penting dalam berbagai hal di dalam perusahaan. The Advisory Mindset (Pendekatan Penasehat) menyajikan cara yang terstruktur bagi karyawan untuk berinteraksi dengan pelanggan dan rekan kerja, memastikan pertukaran informasi yang efisien dan mencegah kesalahpahaman. Dengan menerapkan Pendekatan Penasehat, kita dapat meningkatkan komunikasi, membangun kepercayaan, dan berkontribusi pada kesuksesan organisasi kita.

Makna dari Komunikasi yang Efektif:

1. Mencegah kebingungan dan kesalahpahaman: Pendekatan Penasehat memfasilitasi komunikasi yang jelas dan transparan, mengurangi kebingungan dan penafsiran yang salah di antara pelanggan dan rekan kerja.
2. Mengurangi frustrasi dan ketidakpuasan: Dengan berempati pada pihak lain dan memperkirakan pertanyaan mereka, komunikasi menjadi lebih fokus, mencegah frustrasi yang terkait dengan percakapan yang panjang dan tidak jelas.
3. Membangun kepercayaan dan memperkuat hubungan: Komunikasi yang jelas dan terbuka membantu membangun kepercayaan dan saling menghormati antara pihak-pihak, membuka jalan untuk hubungan yang lebih baik dengan pelanggan dan rekan kerja.
4. Berkontribusi pada kesuksesan organisasi: Komunikasi yang efektif meningkatkan efisiensi, pengambilan keputusan yang lebih baik, dan pelayanan pelanggan yang lebih baik, akhirnya membawa pada peningkatan produktivitas, kepuasan pelanggan, dan pertumbuhan finansial.

Permasalahannya adalah praktik komunikasi yang tidak efisien di dalam organisasi telah mengakibatkan kebingungan, frustrasi, ketidakpuasan, dan ketidakpercayaan di antara pelanggan dan rekan kerja. Akibatnya, produktivitas menurun, kepuasan pelanggan menurun, dan kerugian finansial terjadi.

Dalam upaya meningkatkan komunikasi organisasi, meningkatkan produktivitas, serta meningkatkan kepuasan pelanggan dan hasil keuangan, kita bisa mengadopsi Pendekatan Penasehat sebagai kerangka panduan untuk komunikasi yang efektif.

Pendekatan Penasehat adalah alat berharga bagi karyawan untuk berkomunikasi secara efektif, baik secara internal maupun eksternal. Kerangka kerjanya didasarkan pada lima prinsip: **Rephrase, Empathize, Explain, Recommend, and Anticipate (REERA)**.

1. **Rephrase** atau klarifikasi: Parafrazkan permintaan atau pertanyaan untuk memastikan pemahaman dan membangun kepercayaan. Mintalah klarifikasi jika perlu sebelum merespons.
2. **Empathize** dengan niat: Pahami motivasi di balik permintaan atau pertanyaan untuk memberikan respons yang ditargetkan dan berpikir keras.
3. **Explain** fakta dan isu: Sampaikan informasi yang relevan dan konteks dengan jelas dan transparan, mengasumsikan bahwa pihak lain mungkin kurang memiliki pengetahuan tertentu.
4. **Recommend** rencana atau berikan pendapat: Sarankan solusi yang layak atau langkah tindakan sambil menjaga harapan realistis mengenai hasil dan tanggung jawab.
5. **Anticipate** apa yang akan terjadi selanjutnya: Secara proaktif tangani pertanyaan-pertanyaan lanjutan yang mungkin muncul untuk mencegah kebingungan dan mengatasi semua kekhawatiran.

Menerapkan kerangka REERA memungkinkan karyawan berkomunikasi lebih efektif dan memberikan pelayanan unggul kepada pelanggan dan rekan kerja, menjadikannya alat penting untuk meningkatkan komunikasi di tempat kerja.

Contohnya:

* *Pertanyaan: "Saya butuh lisensi ini siap dalam seminggu. Ada perkembangan apa?"*
* Rephrase: "Anda membutuhkan lisensi Sitefinity untuk disiapkan dan diinstal di backend dalam seminggu mendatang."
* Empathize: "Kami mengerti bahwa acara langsung yang dijadwalkan untuk minggu depan sangat penting, dan proses yang lancar sangatlah penting."
* Explain: "Sayangnya, proses standar pengadaan lisensi membutuhkan waktu 2-3 minggu, dan kami mengandalkan vendor kami untuk mendapatkannya."
* Recommend: "Kami akan menghubungi pihak utama untuk mempercepat proses dan menyelidiki kemungkinan menggunakan lisensi Xtremax sebagai solusi sementara."
* Anticipate: "Saya akan memberikan pembaruan dalam waktu 48 jam berikutnya, memastikan waktu yang cukup untuk penyesuaian yang diperlukan sebelum tanggal penting."

Tentang buku, ini adalah beberapa rekomendasi yang bisa membantu memahami komunikasi yang efektif lebih dalam:

1. **"Crucial Conversations: Tools for Talking When Stakes Are High"** oleh Al Switzler, Joseph Grenny, dan Ron McMillan.
   - **Key Takeaways**: Mengatasi percakapan sulit dengan keterampilan komunikasi yang kuat. Memahami bagaimana menghadapi situasi penting dengan baik.

2. **"How to Win Friends and Influence People"** oleh Dale Carnegie.
   - **Key Takeaways**: Membangun hubungan positif dengan orang lain melalui komunikasi yang mempengaruhi dan menginspirasi.

3. **"Radical Candor: Be a Kick-Ass Boss Without Losing Your Humanity"** oleh Kim Scott.
   - **Key Takeaways**: Menjadi seorang pemimpin yang jujur dan peduli, memadukan umpan balik tegas dengan empati.

4. **"Drive: The Surprising Truth About What Motivates Us"** oleh Daniel H. Pink.
   - **Key Takeaways**: Memahami faktor-faktor yang memotivasi orang, dan bagaimana cara mengkomunikasikan harapan dan tujuan dengan efektif.

5. **"Influence: The Psychology of Persuasion"** oleh Robert B. Cialdini.

PART 1:
**Problem** **:**

During
the requirements gathering, there is often no or very poor
documentation available about existing processes. Uncertainty in the
exiting process or different priorities for different stakeholders often
leads to conflicting requirements. Often the stakeholders and end-users
have a clear picture of how the new solution should look but don’t have
an understanding of what it should do.

**Reality** **:**

* Requirements
  Gathering delayed almost 6 months from the start of the Project. (This
  problem happens since the team didn't set any boundary for each
  requirement.)
* The user keeps throwing any new requirements.
* The team always changing, and the handover will take 2 weeks to attune the scope of the Project.
* Too many users are involved in the Requirements Gathering with Different perspectives.
* The user often changes the needs, even if already documented and starting to develop.
* The
  requirement from the user is quite complex and has the chance we miss
  to fully understand what the user really needs. The solution proposed is
  focused on limited options.
* There is a gap between functional spec documents and what requirements are gathered from users.

**Lesson Learn** **:**

* To
  ensure requirements are full and correct, it’s critical to identify key
  stakeholders and subject matter experts and engage with them directly.
* Doing as much research as possible prior to the engagement will help to make the conversation more structured and insightful.
* The
  business analyst should make sure there is a clear distinction between
  design and functional requirements in their documentation.
* Validate each potential ‘false requirement’.

PART 2:
**Problem** **:**

The requirement spec document is not well documented, which causes a lot of confusion for the team.

**Reality** **:**

* Vague
* Incomplete

**Lesson Learn** **:**

* The RG team needs to document well every endpoint and parameter.
* Be
  clear, Include information about assessments, action taken, outcomes,
  reassessment processes (if necessary), risks, complications, and
  changes.

PART 3:
**Problem** **:**

Many
things weren't settled and we have to propose a workaround for the MVP.
After agreement the MVP, the projects go back to on track and separate
two main focuses as Intranet and Internet.

**Reality** **:**

* The
  proposed project timeline was 9 months for both intranet and internet
  portals. However, the requirements and design phase took 8 months to
  complete this was unexpected till now the project has run for 20 months
  and only the Intranet portion has gone live.
* If
  customers allow remote BA/Development team costs can be kept low.
  However, the requirements phase needs PSC-level intervention to speed
  things up. From a cost sustainability perspective, it is necessary to
  keep resources remote as much as possible.
* Sales/Proposal
  wise the timeline and proposed cost need to meet customer expectations
  to win therefore it is really a question of if we put in a long schedule
  and high cost will be win projects.
* After
  winning projects the PM needs to manage the project such that resource
  cost is kept low (remote) and only ramp up when customers put in the
  same effort to go live.

**Lesson Learn** **:**

* Have a clear outcome and don't over-commit.
* Break down the project.
* Have a start and completion date for each step.

PART 4:
## Background

**There
are countless fields of expertise in this world, like accounting,
business, marketing, retail, and many others and each fields ****have
their own way of thinking, including us as IT people, and our
responsibility is to help those users from the other fields to solve
their business problems. Furthermore, Nimbus Stream Team currently
working directly with application user so that better communication with
non-IT clients would be a very important thing to learn in this current
condition.******

## Our Pain Point

* Hard to communicate
  Because of the different ways of thinking, it is indeed hard to deliver our thought to another field expertise.
* Miscommunication
  It sometimes happen when **we think ****we have already said something clearly, and ****the other person thinks**** they already understand clearly, but it turns out that what we are trying to convey and what they perceive are different**

**This section will cover some small concrete steps to help us minimize the occurrence of this miscommunication.**

## Small Concrete Steps

1. Client Point of View
   The very first step to take is thinking and imagining until all of their business works makes sense to us. To
   be a good developer, we need to understand whatever the client is
   doing, what it is, until it seems as if we can do it. The reason is
   quite clear, that is, we will have intuition about what the client
   needs. For example in general we can make online shop tools application
   better compared to make Permit to Works application, simply because we
   are the user of some of online shop who can imagine the client point of
   view when making the application, but we are not familiar in requesting
   Permit to Works as it's used in some **hazardous industries.
   Our
   first thing to do when gathering the requirement is NOT think about the
   functional specification but to learn about what client usually do,
   including the non-IT related one. If we're talking with non-IT client,
   it's really okay to ask something that isn't related to IT at all. In
   fact, it's much better like that, rather than asking those which are too
   IT. Don't be feature-oriented when we gathering their requirements. Forget
   about "what application we are going to make", focus on "what are they
   doing? If we are going to handle their tasks, what will we do?"
2. Focus on Content
   1. Be careful of dummy data
      Dummy
      does not mean random. A totally random data presented to client can
      make them confused and potentially thinks that our application can't
      deliver what they needs, even though we can just edit the content in 5
      seconds because then again, different field expertise have different way
      of thinking.
   2. Think until all makes sense to client
      To make
      good content, it is much better to make the data as realistic as
      possible. Avoid "test123", "asdf", or "testdummy" data when it comes
      presenting to client, instead research on what the example of their real
      data looks like, to make all makes sense to them. Because when
      presented by realistic data, they can imagine better about how our
      application can solve their real problem.
   3. Know the difference between communicating with team and client
      Sometimes
      there are things that are easy to us but very important to them, and
      there are things that are very hard to us but not too important to them.
      Our tasks is to follow their priority and point of view when it comes
      to communicating, not the other way around.
3. Aware of Terms
   When
   communicating with non-IT client, beware of SQL terms, programming
   language terms that we are often use in internal communication (for
   example we better say "sort" instead of "order by", "edit" instead of
   "update"). We should use terms that are as familiar to them as possible, but after all we should still be careful about using their terms, because if we misuse their terms, they turn out to be confused again

## Conclusion

That's the small concrete steps we can take,
although it will take quite long time to adapt, but it's really worth
to try. To make it simple we should remember to "step into their shoes
to see what happened clearly".

**Bagian 1: Masalah dan Pelajaran yang Didapat**

Masalah:
Saat mengumpulkan persyaratan, seringkali tidak ada atau sangat sedikit dokumentasi yang ada tentang proses yang sudah ada. Ketidakpastian dalam proses yang sudah ada atau prioritas yang berbeda dari berbagai pihak sering kali menghasilkan persyaratan yang saling bertentangan. Kadang stakeholder dan pengguna akhir memiliki gambaran jelas tentang bagaimana solusi baru seharusnya terlihat, tetapi mereka tidak memahami apa yang harus dilakukan.

Pelajaran yang Didapat:
1. Untuk memastikan persyaratan lengkap dan benar, penting untuk mengidentifikasi pihak yang berkepentingan dan ahli di bidang tersebut dan berinteraksi langsung dengan mereka.
2. Lakukan penelitian sebanyak mungkin sebelum terlibat untuk membuat percakapan lebih terstruktur dan bermakna.
3. Pastikan ada perbedaan yang jelas antara desain dan persyaratan fungsional dalam dokumentasi.
4. Validasi setiap potensi 'persyaratan palsu'.

**Bagian 2: Masalah, Kebenaran, dan Pelajaran yang Didapat**

Masalah:
Dokumen spesifikasi persyaratan tidak terdokumentasi dengan baik, yang menyebabkan banyak kebingungan bagi tim.

Kebenaran:
- Kabur
- Tidak lengkap

Pelajaran yang Didapat:
- Tim RG perlu mendokumentasikan dengan baik setiap endpoint dan parameter.
- Jadilah jelas, sertakan informasi tentang penilaian, tindakan yang diambil, hasil, proses reassessment (jika diperlukan), risiko, komplikasi, dan perubahan.

**Bagian 3: Masalah dan Pelajaran yang Didapat**

Masalah:
Banyak hal yang belum diselesaikan dan kami harus mencari solusi sementara untuk MVP (Minimum Viable Product). Setelah setuju dengan MVP, proyek kembali berjalan dan fokus pada dua hal utama, yakni Intranet dan Internet.

Kebenaran:
- Timeline proyek yang diusulkan adalah 9 bulan untuk portal intranet dan internet. Namun, tahap persyaratan dan desain memakan waktu 8 bulan hingga saat ini proyek telah berjalan selama 20 bulan dan hanya bagian Intranet yang sudah diluncurkan.
- Biaya tim BA/Pengembangan yang rendah dapat dijaga jika pelanggan mengizinkan tim yang jauh. Namun, fase persyaratan membutuhkan intervensi tingkat PSC untuk mempercepat proses. Dari perspektif keberlanjutan biaya, penting untuk menjaga sumber daya tetap jauh sebisa mungkin.
- Dari segi penjualan/proposal, timeline dan biaya yang diusulkan perlu memenuhi harapan pelanggan untuk memenangkan proyek, jadi pertanyaannya sebenarnya adalah apakah kita memasukkan jadwal panjang dan biaya tinggi akan memenangkan proyek.
- Setelah memenangkan proyek, PM perlu mengelola proyek sehingga biaya sumber daya tetap rendah (jauh) dan hanya meningkatkan ketika pelanggan juga berusaha untuk meluncurkan.

Pelajaran yang Didapat:
- Tetapkan hasil yang jelas dan jangan berlebihan.
- Membagi proyek menjadi langkah-langkah.
- Tetapkan tanggal mulai dan selesai untuk setiap langkah.

**Bagian 4: Latar Belakang dan Langkah-langkah Kecil yang Konkret**

Latar Belakang:
Ada banyak bidang keahlian di dunia ini, seperti akuntansi, bisnis, pemasaran, ritel, dan lain-lain, dan setiap bidang memiliki cara berpikirnya sendiri, termasuk kita sebagai orang IT. Tanggung jawab kita adalah membantu pengguna dari bidang lain untuk menyelesaikan masalah bisnis mereka. Lebih jauh lagi, Tim Nimbus Stream saat ini bekerja langsung dengan pengguna aplikasi, sehingga komunikasi yang lebih baik dengan klien non-IT sangat penting untuk dipelajari dalam kondisi saat ini.

Poin Kesulitan Kita:
- Sulit untuk berkomunikasi karena perbedaan cara berpikir, sulit untuk menyampaikan pikiran kita kepada ahli dari bidang lain.
- Terkadang terjadi kesalahpahaman saat kita berpikir kita sudah menjelaskan sesuatu dengan jelas, tapi orang lain berpikir mereka sudah memahaminya dengan jelas, namun ternyata apa yang kita coba sampaikan dan apa yang mereka pahami berbeda.

Langkah-langkah Kecil yang Konkret:
1. Sudut Pandang Klien
   Langkah pertama yang sangat penting adalah memikirkan dan membayangkan hingga semua pekerjaan bisnis mereka masuk akal bagi kita. Untuk menjadi pengembang yang baik, kita perlu memahami apa yang klien lakukan, bagaimana caranya, hingga terasa seolah kita bisa melakukannya. Alasannya cukup jelas, yaitu kita akan memiliki intuisi tentang apa yang klien butuhkan.
   Misalnya, secara umum kita bisa membuat aplikasi alat toko online lebih baik dibandingkan membuat aplikasi izin kerja, hanya karena kita adalah pengguna beberapa toko online dan bisa membayangkan sudut pandang klien saat membuat aplikasi, tapi kita tidak terbiasa dalam mengajukan izin kerja seperti yang digunakan dalam beberapa industri berbahaya.
   Langkah pertama kita saat mengumpulkan persyaratan adalah JANGAN berpikir tentang spesifikasi fungsional, tetapi mempelajari apa yang biasanya dilakukan klien, termasuk yang tidak berhubungan dengan IT sama sekali. Jika kita berbicara dengan klien non-IT, sangat baik untuk bertanya tentang hal yang sama sekali tidak berhubungan dengan IT. Bahkan, itu lebih baik daripada bertanya tentang hal-hal yang terlalu berhubungan dengan IT. Jangan terlalu fokus pada fitur saat mengumpulkan persyaratan mereka. Lupakan tentang "aplikasi apa yang akan kita buat", fokus pada "apa yang mereka lakukan? Jika kita akan menangani tugas-tugas mereka, apa yang akan kita lakukan?"
2. Fokus pada Konten
   1. Hati-hati dengan data palsu
      Palsu
      tidak berarti acak. Data yang sep

enuhnya acak yang disajikan kepada
      klien bisa membuat mereka bingung dan berpotensi berpikir bahwa
      aplikasi kita tidak dapat memberikan apa yang mereka butuhkan,
      meskipun sebenarnya kita hanya perlu mengedit konten dalam 5 detik,
      karena pada akhirnya, ahli di bidang yang berbeda berpikir dengan cara
      yang berbeda.
   2. Pikirkan hingga semuanya masuk akal bagi klien
      Untuk
      membuat konten yang baik, jauh lebih baik membuat data se-realistis
      mungkin. Hindari data "test123", "asdf", atau "testdummy" saat
      disajikan kepada klien, sebaliknya, cari tahu contoh data nyata mereka
      seperti apa, agar semuanya masuk akal bagi mereka. Karena saat
      disajikan dengan data yang realistis, mereka dapat membayangkan lebih
      baik tentang bagaimana aplikasi kita dapat menyelesaikan masalah
      sebenarnya.
   3. Pahami perbedaan antara berkomunikasi dengan tim dan klien
      Terkadang
      ada hal-hal yang mudah bagi kita tetapi sangat penting bagi mereka, dan
      ada hal-hal yang sangat sulit bagi kita tetapi tidak terlalu penting
      bagi mereka. Tugas kita adalah mengikuti prioritas dan sudut pandang
      mereka dalam berkomunikasi, bukan sebaliknya.

3. Sadar akan Istilah
   Saat berkomunikasi dengan klien non-IT, berhati-hatilah dengan istilah SQL, istilah bahasa pemrograman yang sering kita gunakan dalam komunikasi internal (misalnya, lebih baik katakan "sort" daripada "order by", "edit" daripada "update"). Kami harus menggunakan istilah yang sesuai dengan pengalaman mereka, tetapi tetap berhati-hati dalam menggunakan istilah mereka, karena jika kami salah menggunakan istilah mereka, mereka bisa bingung lagi.

**Kesimpulan:**

Itu dia langkah-langkah kecil yang bisa kita ambil, meskipun perlu waktu yang cukup lama untuk beradaptasi, tetapi sangat layak untuk dicoba. Ingatlah untuk "melangkah ke dalam sepatu mereka untuk melihat apa yang terjadi dengan jelas".

Tentu, oniichan! Berikut aku sajikan kembali dalam bentuk paragraf yang lebih panjang dan mudah dibaca dalam bahasa Indonesia. Aku juga akan membaginya menjadi beberapa bagian berdasarkan topiknya yang semuanya berkaitan dengan komunikasi.

**Bagian 1: Masalah dan Pelajaran yang Didapat**

Saat bekerja pada proyek, sering kali muncul tantangan dalam mengumpulkan persyaratan yang cukup dan tepat. Dokumentasi yang kurang lengkap atau bahkan tidak ada tentang proses yang sudah ada bisa menyebabkan ketidakpastian. Hal ini bisa semakin rumit karena setiap pihak yang terlibat memiliki perspektif dan prioritas yang berbeda, yang pada akhirnya dapat menyebabkan konflik dalam persyaratan yang diajukan. Terkadang, para pihak yang terlibat memiliki gambaran yang jelas tentang bagaimana solusi baru seharusnya terlihat, tetapi mereka mungkin tidak sepenuhnya memahami apa yang harus dicapai.

Namun, dari masalah tersebut, kami telah belajar beberapa pelajaran berharga. Pertama, untuk memastikan bahwa persyaratan yang dikumpulkan benar-benar lengkap dan akurat, sangat penting untuk mengidentifikasi pihak-pihak yang berkepentingan dan ahli di bidang tersebut, dan berinteraksi secara langsung dengan mereka. Selanjutnya, melakukan sebanyak mungkin penelitian sebelum terlibat dalam percakapan dapat membantu membangun kerangka percakapan yang lebih terstruktur dan mendalam. Penting juga untuk menciptakan pemisahan yang jelas antara persyaratan desain dan persyaratan fungsional dalam dokumen yang dihasilkan. Dan yang tak kalah pentingnya adalah memvalidasi setiap potensi 'persyaratan palsu', yang artinya memastikan bahwa setiap persyaratan yang diajukan memang diperlukan dan relevan.

**Bagian 2: Masalah, Kebenaran, dan Pelajaran yang Didapat**

Saat berbicara tentang proses komunikasi dalam pengumpulan persyaratan, seringkali tim menghadapi kendala dalam mendokumentasikan persyaratan dengan baik. Dokumen spesifikasi persyaratan sering kali tampak kabur dan kurang rinci, yang pada akhirnya dapat menyebabkan kebingungan di antara anggota tim.

Namun, dari situasi ini, kita dapat menarik beberapa pelajaran berharga. Pertama-tama, tim pengumpulan persyaratan perlu menjaga agar setiap detail tentang setiap endpoint dan parameter didokumentasikan dengan baik. Selanjutnya, penting untuk berbicara dengan jelas dan tuntas dalam dokumen tersebut. Termasuk informasi tentang evaluasi, tindakan yang diambil, hasil yang dicapai, proses reevaluasi (jika diperlukan), risiko, komplikasi yang mungkin muncul, dan perubahan yang terjadi selama proses. Dengan mendokumentasikan secara lengkap dan terperinci, kita dapat meminimalkan potensi kebingungan dan mencegah kesalahpahaman di antara anggota tim.

**Bagian 3: Masalah dan Pelajaran yang Didapat**

Saat mempertimbangkan efisiensi dalam menjalankan proyek, terkadang kita perlu mencari solusi sementara untuk mencapai tujuan awal. Dalam beberapa situasi, proposal proyek yang awalnya direncanakan dapat mengalami perubahan atau penyesuaian. Sebagai contoh, dalam sebuah proyek yang merencanakan pengembangan portal intranet dan internet, terjadi penundaan dalam tahap pengumpulan persyaratan yang menyebabkan perubahan jadwal keseluruhan. Namun, setelah mencapai kesepakatan mengenai Minimum Viable Product (MVP), proyek kembali berjalan dengan fokus pada dua tujuan utama: portal intranet dan portal internet.

Namun, dari pengalaman ini, kita dapat menarik beberapa pelajaran penting. Pertama, memiliki hasil yang jelas dan realistis merupakan langkah awal yang sangat penting. Dalam menjalankan proyek, perlu memecahnya menjadi langkah-langkah yang lebih kecil dan teratur, serta menetapkan tanggal mulai dan selesai untuk setiap tahapan. Kedua, dalam merencanakan dan mengelola sumber daya proyek, penting untuk mempertimbangkan kembali apakah penggunaan tim jarak jauh (remote) adalah pilihan yang efektif, serta bagaimana efektivitas dalam penggunaan sumber daya tersebut. Terakhir, berkomitmen untuk menjaga kualitas dan keberlanjutan biaya proyek melalui manajemen yang cermat adalah suatu keharusan.

**Bagian 4: Latar Belakang, Kesulitan, dan Langkah-langkah Kecil yang Konkret**

Setiap bidang memiliki cara berpikir yang unik, termasuk dalam dunia IT di mana kita berada. Sebagai bagian dari Tim Nimbus Stream, interaksi langsung dengan pengguna aplikasi non-IT menjadi semakin penting dalam konteks saat ini. Kendala yang sering dihadapi adalah kesulitan berkomunikasi dan kesalahpahaman saat berinteraksi dengan pengguna dari berbagai latar belakang.

Namun, kita dapat mengambil beberapa langkah konkret untuk meminimalkan potensi kesalahan komunikasi. Pertama, kita perlu mengenali dan merasakan sudut pandang klien secara mendalam. Ini berarti kita harus benar-benar memahami apa yang mereka lakukan dalam bisnis mereka, hingga kita dapat dengan mudah merasakan dan berpikir seperti mereka. Ini akan membantu kita memahami kebutuhan mereka dengan lebih baik dan menghadirkan solusi yang lebih relevan. Selanjutnya, kita perlu fokus pada konten yang akan disajikan kepada klien. Penting untuk menghindari penggunaan data palsu atau acak, dan sebaliknya menciptakan data yang realistis dan relevan untuk klien. Ini akan membantu mereka lebih mudah memahami bagaimana aplikasi kita dapat mengatasi masalah yang sebenarnya mereka hadapi.

Selain itu, kita perlu waspada terhadap istilah yang digunakan dalam komunikasi. Saat berbicara dengan klien non-IT, kita perlu menghindari penggunaan istilah teknis yang mungkin tidak dikenali oleh mereka. Alih-alih, kita perlu menggunakan istilah yang lebih akr

ab dan dipahami oleh klien. Dengan begitu, kita dapat menjaga klaritas dan pemahaman yang lebih baik dalam komunikasi dengan klien.

Kesimpulannya, menghadapi tantangan komunikasi dalam berbagai situasi adalah hal yang umum, tetapi dengan memahami prinsip-prinsip dan langkah-langkah konkret yang telah dinyatakan di atas, kita dapat meningkatkan kemampuan kita dalam berkomunikasi secara efektif dan menjalankan proyek dengan lebih baik.

what does stakeholder means as general and as specific in IT project?

Sure thing, oniichan! 🌟

**In General:**
In a general context, a stakeholder refers to any individual, group, or entity that has an interest, involvement, or concern in a particular project, organization, or initiative. Stakeholders can be directly or indirectly affected by the outcomes and decisions related to the project. They can include individuals within or outside the organization, such as employees, customers, suppliers, investors, regulators, community members, and more. Stakeholders often have varying levels of influence, and their perspectives and needs are taken into consideration to ensure successful project outcomes.

**In IT Project:**
In the context of an IT project, stakeholders encompass various individuals and groups who are associated with or impacted by the project's design, development, implementation, and operation. Here are some specific examples of stakeholders in an IT project:

1. **Business Users:** These are individuals or teams within the organization who will use the IT solution to achieve their business goals and tasks. They provide insights into the functional requirements and usability of the system.

2. **Project Sponsor:** The person or group that provides the financial resources, support, and overall direction for the project. The project sponsor is usually a senior executive or leader within the organization.

3. **Project Manager:** Responsible for planning, executing, and closing the project. The project manager ensures that the project meets its goals, stays within scope, and adheres to timelines.

4. **Development Team:** IT professionals who design, develop, test, and implement the IT solution. This may include software developers, programmers, and system analysts.

5. **Testing Team:** Individuals who perform quality assurance and testing to ensure that the IT solution functions correctly and meets the specified requirements.

6. **End Users:** The people who will directly use the IT solution once it's implemented. Their feedback and needs are crucial for designing user-friendly interfaces and functionalities.

7. **IT Support Team:** Responsible for maintaining, troubleshooting, and providing technical support for the IT solution post-implementation.

8. **Regulatory Authorities:** If the IT project involves compliance with specific regulations or industry standards, regulatory authorities become stakeholders with a vested interest in the project's adherence to those requirements.

9. **External Vendors/Partners:** Third-party suppliers or vendors who provide software, hardware, or services for the project.

10. **Customers/Clients:** If the IT solution is customer-facing, customers or clients using the solution become stakeholders, influencing its design and functionality.

Understanding and managing the needs, expectations, and contributions of these stakeholders is crucial for the success of an IT project. Effective communication, collaboration, and engagement with stakeholders help ensure that the project aligns with business objectives and delivers value to all parties involved.
translate to bahasa

Tentu, oniichan! 🌟

**Secara Umum:**
Secara umum, pemangku kepentingan (stakeholder) merujuk kepada individu, kelompok, atau entitas yang memiliki minat, keterlibatan, atau kepentingan dalam suatu proyek, organisasi, atau inisiatif tertentu. Pemangku kepentingan bisa secara langsung atau tidak langsung terpengaruh oleh hasil dan keputusan yang terkait dengan proyek tersebut. Mereka dapat mencakup individu di dalam atau di luar organisasi, seperti karyawan, pelanggan, pemasok, investor, regulator, anggota masyarakat, dan lainnya. Pemangku kepentingan sering memiliki tingkat pengaruh yang berbeda, dan pandangan serta kebutuhan mereka diperhitungkan untuk memastikan hasil proyek yang sukses.

**Dalam Proyek IT:**
Dalam konteks proyek IT, pemangku kepentingan mencakup berbagai individu dan kelompok yang terkait atau terpengaruh oleh desain, pengembangan, implementasi, dan operasionalisasi solusi IT. Berikut adalah beberapa contoh khusus pemangku kepentingan dalam proyek IT:

1. **Pengguna Bisnis:** Ini adalah individu atau tim di dalam organisasi yang akan menggunakan solusi IT untuk mencapai tujuan dan tugas bisnis mereka. Mereka memberikan wawasan tentang persyaratan fungsional dan kegunaan sistem.

2. **Pengelola Proyek:** Orang atau kelompok yang menyediakan sumber daya keuangan, dukungan, dan arahan keseluruhan untuk proyek. Pengelola proyek biasanya adalah eksekutif senior atau pemimpin dalam organisasi.

3. **Manajer Proyek:** Bertanggung jawab untuk merencanakan, melaksanakan, dan menyelesaikan proyek. Manajer proyek memastikan bahwa proyek mencapai tujuannya, tetap dalam lingkup, dan mematuhi jadwal waktu.

4. **Tim Pengembangan:** Profesional IT yang merancang, mengembangkan, menguji, dan menerapkan solusi IT. Ini bisa termasuk pengembang perangkat lunak, programmer, dan analis sistem.

5. **Tim Pengujian:** Individu yang melakukan jaminan kualitas dan pengujian untuk memastikan bahwa solusi IT berfungsi dengan benar dan memenuhi persyaratan yang ditentukan.

6. **Pengguna Akhir:** Orang-orang yang akan secara langsung menggunakan solusi IT setelah diimplementasikan. Umpan balik dan kebutuhan mereka penting untuk merancang antarmuka dan fungsionalitas yang ramah pengguna.

7. **Tim Dukungan IT:** Bertanggung jawab untuk menjaga, mengatasi masalah, dan memberikan dukungan teknis untuk solusi IT setelah implementasi.

8. **Otoritas Regulasi:** Jika proyek IT melibatkan kepatuhan terhadap peraturan khusus atau standar industri, otoritas regulasi menjadi pemangku kepentingan yang memiliki minat dalam kepatuhan proyek terhadap persyaratan tersebut.

9. **Vendor/Mitra Eksternal:** Pemasok atau vendor pihak ketiga yang menyediakan perangkat lunak, perangkat keras, atau layanan untuk proyek.

10. **Pelanggan/Klien:** Jika solusi IT menghadap pelanggan, pelanggan yang menggunakan solusi tersebut menjadi pemangku kepentingan, memengaruhi desain dan fungsionalitasnya.

Memahami dan mengelola kebutuhan, harapan, dan kontribusi pemangku kepentingan ini sangat penting untuk kesuksesan proyek IT. Komunikasi, kolaborasi, dan keterlibatan yang efektif dengan pemangku kepentingan membantu memastikan bahwa proyek sejalan dengan tujuan bisnis dan memberikan nilai kepada semua pihak yang terlibat.

I apologize for any confusion, oniichan, but there's a slight issue in your code. In Golang, the short declaration `:=` is used for declaring and initializing variables within a function scope. For variables declared using the `var` keyword, you should use the assignment operator `=` for initialization.

Here's the corrected version of your code:

```go
package main

func main() {
    // Deklarasi variabel
    var permenDiTanganKiri int
    permenDiTanganKanan := 3
}
```

In this version, I've used `var` to declare `permenDiTanganKiri` and the short declaration `:=` for `permenDiTanganKanan` since it's within a function scope.

I'm glad you're eager to learn, oniichan! However, in Golang, you can't redeclare a variable using the same name within the same scope. The `:=` short declaration syntax is used for declaring and initializing new variables.

If you want to reassign a new value to an existing variable, you should use the assignment (`=`) operator like this:

```go
package main

func main() {
    number := 42
    number = 100 // Reassigning a new value to the existing variable
}
```

But you can't use `:=` to declare `number` again with a different type. Golang is statically typed, so once a variable's type is determined, it can't be changed within the same scope.

If you have any more questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask, oniichan! *pats head*

I appreciate your curiosity, oniichan, but I have to correct you again. In Golang, you cannot assign a value of one type to a variable that has been declared with a different type. Doing so would result in a compilation error.

In your provided code:

```go
package main

func main() {
    number := 42
    number = "Hello, world!" // This line will cause a compilation error
}
```

The assignment `number = "Hello, world!"` will not compile because you're trying to assign a string to a variable that was initially declared as an integer (`number := 42`).

Golang is statically typed, which means once you've declared the type of a variable, you must stick to that type for the variable's lifetime within that scope.

If you have further questions or need more clarification, don't hesitate to ask, oniichan! *pats head*
